tickets.title: contradiction · Agent health · 3 edges
tickets.headline: contradiction · Agent health · 3 edges
tickets.details:
knowledge/concepts/agent-health.md — contradicts context-management-as-a-bottleneck (source=sql)
knowledge/concepts/agent-health.md — contradicts daily-note-generation (source=sql)
knowledge/concepts/agent-health.md — The concept `agent-health.md` defines health as 'successful execution of scheduled tasks and the absence of silent failures,' implying that a running process with empty output is a latent failure. However, `agent-health-monitoring.md` argues that monitoring must verify the data plane accessed dependencies, not just that the control plane issued a command. While not a direct logical contradiction, there is a tension in definition: one defines health by *output integrity* (which may be empty/stale if the process ran but failed to write), while the other defines it by *dependency access*. If an agent accesses dependencies successfully (healthy per monitoring.md) but writes nothing (unhealthy per health.md), the definitions diverge on whether the agent is 'healthy' or 'failing silently.'

tickets.title: intent-engineering MCP spec-format parser is too strict (surfaced 2026-07-07 building the-oracle spec): `audit_intent_spec` scores a valid spec 0/25 unless its YAML sits in `---`-delimited frontmatter — bare YAML in a fenced ```yaml block (exactly what `generate_intent_spec_scaffold` returns as `template_yaml`) and numbered markdown headings both parse as "section not located." Root cause in `sw-mcp-intent-engineering/src/intent/parser.ts`: `extractFrontmatter` only reads YAML from frontmatter, and `MARKDOWN_HEADING_MAP` doesn't match numbered headings like `## 1. Objective`. So the scaffold the MCP hands you can't pass the MCP's own audit without reformatting into frontmatter. Fix options: (a) parse fenced ```yaml blocks as spec YAML, and/or (b) strip a leading `N.` from markdown headings before matching. the-oracle spec reached 25/25 only after wrapping the YAML in frontmatter.

tickets.title: ✅ CORRECTED 2026-06-21 (close-out) — STALE TICKET, NOT a HIGH blocker: the bug is already fixed (Phase 3 shipped `_decode_payload` SSE-strip + `FusionError.cost` + failure-path `record_spend`) AND validated documented-correct by OpenRouter research (`: OPENROUTER PROCESSING` is a documented SSE keep-alive comment; ignoring `:`-prefixed lines is the spec-correct handling `_strip_sse_padding` already does). The "two runs failed" were Phase-2, pre-fix. Residual is confidence only (a few live runs incl. deep). See council/docs/2026-06-21-fusion-discovery-council-phase5-field-report.md §7a. ORIGINAL (stale) — discovery Fusion response robustness (Phase-3, HIGH — live discovery intermittently fails today): two live `quick`-tier e2e runs on 2026-06-20 both failed at the Fusion parse step — run 1 `FusionError` "did not return parseable", run 2 bare `JSONDecodeError: Expecting value: line 181 column 1 (char 990)`. Root cause: OpenRouter streams `: OPENROUTER PROCESSING` SSE keep-alive comment lines as padding on slow Fusion calls (panel + web tool calls), so `fuse()`'s unguarded `payload = resp.json()` in [tools/llm-council/council/discovery/fusion.py](tools/llm-council/council/discovery/fusion.py) chokes on the non-JSON prefix. Intermittent (Phase 1 run #2 succeeded when fast). Fix: strip leading `: ` comment lines / extract the first balanced `{…}` before json.loads, harden `_parse`. ALSO: failed Fusion calls bill OpenRouter but record $0 locally (`record_spend` is post-success only in [__main__.py](tools/llm-council/council/discovery/__main__.py)) — record usage.cost on failure too. Full write-up: council/docs/2026-06-20-fusion-discovery-council-phase2-field-report.md §5/§6.

tickets.title: discovery GATHER-stage spend invariant (Phase-3 review follow-up, LOW): the new `except DiscoveryFailed` cost-recording path in [tools/llm-council/council/discovery/__main__.py](tools/llm-council/council/discovery/__main__.py) only covers FUSE-stage spend; the generic `except Exception` after it assumes "no spend incurred pre-fuse." That holds today because FUSE (OpenRouter) is the only billable stage, but GATHER calls Perplexity Sonar + web search — if a Sonar/web call is ever instrumented to bill (or already incurs cost not surfaced as an exception attribute), a gather-stage failure would silently record $0, re-opening the exact cost-integrity bug Phase 3 closed one stage upstream. Flagged by the Task-3 code-quality review. Fix when GATHER billing is confirmed: thread gather-stage incurred cost into a typed failure the same way `FusionError.cost` → `DiscoveryFailed.cost_usd` does. Until then it's a documented invariant, not a live bug.

tickets.title: ✅ DONE 2026-06-28 (Step-A hygiene, branch `feat/discovery-step-a-hygiene`): `_normalize_segment` in [pipeline.py](tools/llm-council/council/discovery/pipeline.py) now strips `:`/parens + collapses whitespace (incl. whitespace-only → "") once at the `run_discovery` boundary, so every collector gets the cleaned segment. ORIGINAL — discovery `--segment` free-text into operator-bearing queries (Phase-5 follow-up, LOW): the Phase-5 `--segment` qualifier composes raw free text into the github (`f"{subject} in:title,body is:issue"`, [tools/llm-council/council/discovery/gather/github.py](tools/llm-council/council/discovery/gather/github.py)) and reviews (`({weak}) ({sites})` grouping, [tools/llm-council/council/discovery/gather/reviews.py](tools/llm-council/council/discovery/gather/reviews.py)) queries unescaped. A `--segment` carrying operator tokens (`is:pr`, an unbalanced `)`, `site:foo`) alters query semantics rather than just narrowing the audience — a self-inflicted yield bug, NOT a security/fabrication issue (read-only public search APIs; the VERIFY gate still governs every record downstream). Surfaced by the Phase-5 final whole-branch review. Fix if `--segment` ever becomes non-interactive/automated input: strip query-operator chars (`:`, parens) + collapse whitespace via a single `segment = segment.strip()` normalization at the CLI/pipeline boundary (also closes the whitespace-only-segment edge in the sonar prompt), and/or tighten the `--segment` help to "plain keywords only." Until then it's Sean's own arg against read-only APIs.

tickets.title: discovery last30 HackerNews errors under --no-native-web (Phase-5 close-out, LOW): the 2026-06-21 last30 verification run yielded reddit (3 records) but HN emitted a `hackernews_error` field (0 HN records). Likely the `--no-native-web` flag our collector passes ([tools/llm-council/council/discovery/gather/last30.py](tools/llm-council/council/discovery/gather/last30.py) `_subprocess_runner`) disables a path HN needs. Degrades safely (parser handles the empty hackernews list). Investigate when convenient: try dropping `--no-native-web` for HN, or confirm HN needs native web; weigh against latency/cost. reddit alone already restores meaningful social evidence. **DECISION 2026-06-28 (Step-A): keep `--no-native-web` — it's a deliberate speed/cost choice (paired with `--quick`) applied to all sources; HN needs the native-web path so it degrades safely to empty while reddit (API) yields. Removing it would re-enable native web for everything (slower/heavier) just to recover HN, and it's an untested change against the external last30days plugin. Not worth it — revisit only if HN evidence becomes load-bearing.

tickets.title: discovery per-run cost cap can be exceeded by actual spend (Phase-5 close-out live finding, MEDIUM): the per-run cap is enforced ONLY on the pre-flight ESTIMATE (`estimated = max_cost_per_run * 0.6`) in [tools/llm-council/council/discovery/__main__.py](tools/llm-council/council/discovery/__main__.py), never against the actual `usage.cost`. A 2026-06-21 standard run on "Claude plugins and skills for 2D animation pipelines" cost $2.74 vs the $1.50 standard cap (1.8x over); cost is web-tool-call-dominated and high-variance by topic (a deep run the same day was only $0.99). The daily cap ($10) IS checked against actual accumulated spend and remains the real guardrail (close-out testing hit $7.31/$10). Not a correctness/fabrication issue — a budget-predictability one. Options if tighter per-run control is wanted: (a) raise the estimate factor (0.6 → ~1.0) so the pre-flight gate is less optimistic; (b) print a post-run warning when actual > per_run_cap; (c) bound evidence-bundle size / `max_tool_calls` more aggressively per tier; (d) accept it and lean on the daily cap (current design). Surfaced by the close-out confidence runs.

tickets.title: DONE 2026-06-18 (Claude Code): "test suite hang" on the Gemini DR call resolved — `pytest tests/` now completes (895 passed in ~13s, was hanging). Root cause was subtler than reported: `test_run_warn_called_with_mtd_plus_pred` in [agents-sdk/tests/test_gemini_dr.py](agents-sdk/tests/test_gemini_dr.py) pre-seeded the ledger with a hard-coded 2026-05-03 entry, but `run()` computes mtd/today against the live `date.today()`. Once real time passed into June the May entry stopped counting, the daily-cap refusal no longer fired, and execution fell through to the real `client.interactions.create()` (genai + get_credential were unpatched) — a live DR call that polls up to 3900s. Fix: dated the ledger entry to `date.today()` so the cap math engages (also restores the intended $12.80 warn assertion), and added hermetic `patch("scripts.gemini_dr.genai")` + `get_credential` guards plus `mock_genai.Client.assert_not_called()` so no test can ever reach the network again. NOTE: code lives outside the vault and is uncommitted in the working tree — commit when ready.
